name: Starlark
description: A Rust implementation of Starlark, a deterministic Python-inspired configuration language used by Bazel, Buck, and Buck2

language-family.comment: Starlark is a deterministic language inspired by Python3

typing.comment: Dynamic typing with optional runtime-checked type annotations

paradigm.comment: Imperative with first-class functions; no user-defined types or OOP

closures.comment: Nested functions capture variables from enclosing scopes

pattern-matching.comment: No pattern matching constructs in the language

error-handling.comment: Uses starlark::Result with anyhow integration for error propagation

async-await.comment: No async/await support; language is designed for deterministic execution

iterators-generators.comment: Iteration via comprehensions, for loops, range(), enumerate(), zip(); no generators

module-system.comment: Load statement for importing code from other modules

standard-library.comment: Built-in types (str, list, dict, set) and functions (len, range, sorted, min, max, etc.)

execution-strategy.comment: Compiles to bytecode VM; heap-allocated values with garbage collection

compilation-model.comment: Runtime evaluation or precompilation to AST; FrozenModule for reusable compiled code

hot-reloading.comment: Modules can be reloaded; mutable Module converts to FrozenModule

repl-support.comment: Separate starlark-repl crate provides interactive evaluation

debugging-support.comment: Debug module with DAP (Debug Adapter Protocol) support

source-maps.comment: Error types include source location information

startup-time.comment: Lightweight bytecode VM, no JIT warmup

execution-speed.comment: Bytecode VM optimized for configuration evaluation rather than compute-heavy workloads

memory-overhead.comment: Efficient implementation designed for build systems with large dependency graphs

garbage-collection.comment: Heap-based allocation with freeze mechanism for immutable values

pure-rust.comment: Pure Rust implementation with no C/C++ dependencies

api-safety.comment: Unsafe contained internally; public API is safe Rust

idiomatic-api.comment: Proc-macros (#[starlark_module], #[starlark_value]), builder patterns, good error types

serde-integration.comment: serde/serde_json integration for JSON serialization; to_json() method available

async-compatible.comment: Synchronous execution; language designed for deterministic build configuration

function-registration.comment: #[starlark_module] attribute macro for exposing Rust functions

type-mapping.comment: StarlarkValue trait with automatic conversions; unpack methods for type extraction

no-std-compatible.comment: Requires std; no no_std support documented

wasm-compatible.comment: starlark_js_example directory suggests WASM experimentation; not primary target

blank-slate-mode.comment: GlobalsBuilder allows full control over what symbols are available

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory; heap-isolated execution

cpu-limits.comment: Evaluation can be interrupted; instruction counting possible

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O; deterministic by design

capability-security.comment: GlobalsBuilder controls exactly what functions are available to scripts

default-deny.comment: Scripts can only access explicitly registered functions via GlobalsBuilder

share-data.comment: Values are heap-allocated; data passed via cloning or Value wrappers

custom-types.comment: StarlarkValue trait for custom types; #[starlark_value] derive macro

operator-overloading.comment: Custom types can implement operators via StarlarkValue trait methods

method-syntax.comment: Dot notation for methods on custom types

callbacks-events.comment: Functions can be passed as values and called from Rust

package-manager.comment: No package ecosystem; designed for embedding in build systems

editor-support.comment: starlark_lsp crate provides LSP server; syntax highlighting available

example-projects.comment: Primary use is Buck2 build system; also used for configuration and tooling

maintenance-status.comment: Facebook/Meta backed; 75 contributors, regular releases, used in production for Buck2

documentation-quality.comment: 100% documented API with examples; lacks comprehensive tutorial/book

bus-factor.comment: Maintained by Facebook/Meta; 75 contributors, used in Buck2

maturity.comment: Production use in Buck2; first released 2018, version 0.13.0

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed for build system configuration; deterministic execution for rules
